tipRules.tip: KVD: 'I caught half a dozen on a jerkbait in 41°F water. When you find one, they're not going to be alone — there's going to be more.'

KVD: 'A Rage Swimmer scuffed along the bottom is deadly in cold water. Just scoot it along — looks like a perch, bluegill, or crawfish. Fish it DOWN on the bottom, slow hopping. Night and day difference in bites vs. swimming it through the water column.'
KVD: 'When that water gets into the mid to upper 40s in March, you're going to start catching them with a Thunder Cricket. That's when fish move out of wintering areas and start patrolling flats.'
KVD: 'Red Eye Shad is just dynamite when fish start moving out of the basin onto flats. That's when spring fishing gets really good.'
KVD: 'I threw the Ned rig out and immediately caught one on that in 43°F water.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'In cold water (40s°F), bass are where the food is — locked into winter areas. Look for depressions, little ditches, anywhere slightly deeper with warm water around it. Graph for bluegill concentrations — if you see wall-to-wall bluegill, the bass will be there.'

KVD: 'Look for areas with dark bottom — dead vegetation, pad stems, rocks. Sun radiates anything dark on the bottom. Even in cold water, depth changes, depressions, and little ditches hold fish. Same principle north and south.'
KVD: 'Bass were locked into one corner of the hole where wind was pushing in. Anything that creates current, a funnel, or an edge leading onto a flat — that's where fish stack in cold water.'

KVD: 'The Rage Swimmer on a jig head, scooting along bottom, kicks up mud and sand. In clear northern lakes, all forage lives on the bottom — perch, bluegill, crawfish. Swim it through the column only when bluegill are suspended.'

KVD: 'For cold water swimbaits and finesse — Custom Lite 200 series shallow spool reel with 8lb braid + fluoro leader. Shallow spool manages line perfectly, no wind knots. Saves line since bass won't strip 100 yards.'
